timeline.label: Financial Times reported QuidelOrtho exploring ~$1.5B sale of point-of-care unit to cut merger debt; unconfirmed catalyst.
Q1 FY2026 earnings: revenue $619.8M (−10.5% YoY); FY26 guide flat sales $2.70–2.75B and EBITDA $615–630M.
FY2025 10-K filed: revenue $2,730.2M (−1.9% YoY); adj EBITDA $597M (+240 bps margin); net debt ~$2.38B.
CEO Brian Blaser open-market purchase ~23,500 shares (~$501K); insider buying cluster through Feb 2026.
U.S. donor screening business exit in progress; FY2025 revenue $52.6M (−54% YoY).
LEX / Savanna molecular platform launch investment; company guided ~$50M launch drag for 2026.
Post-COVID respiratory normalization continued; POC revenue volatility persists.
Quidel and Ortho Clinical Diagnostics merger closed; combined leverage ~$3.8B merger debt overhang.
Post-merger leadership integration; Brian Blaser CEO through refresh period.
Quidel acquired Ortho Clinical Diagnostics for ~$3.8B, creating QuidelOrtho and heavy term debt.

industry.structure: Consolidated diagnostics oligopoly (Abbott, Roche, Danaher, Siemens) with high recurring consumable mix on installed instruments; reimbursement and hospital budget pressure cap pricing.
industry.share_shift: Post-COVID POC respiratory volumes normalized down; central lab and transfusion franchises steadier; molecular platforms (LEX, GeneXpert) compete for hospital capital budgets.
industry.trend: Margin expansion via cost programs offsetting flat revenue; strategic portfolio simplification (POC sale rumors, donor screening exit) to address merger leverage.
